free_text: Cadet raised a concern that does not fit a specific category but left staff feeling that it should be recorded and monitored. At present there is no indication of immediate risk, but the situation will be monitored and support options explained to the cadet.

Another cadet reported that this cadet has spoken about wanting to travel to conflict zones and ‘fight for a cause’. There are indicators of immediate or very serious risk and urgent safeguarding action is required in line with policy.

Cadet asked general questions about whether certain traditional practices on girls are allowed in this country and appeared worried. Further conversation with the cadet and parents/carers is needed to understand the situation and decide on next steps.

Staff were told that the cadet has been missing from home overnight on several occasions and returns with unexplained money or items. The information suggests a significant safeguarding concern and the case requires prompt discussion with the safeguarding lead.

Cadet reported being added to group chats where explicit or disturbing content is shared, and feels pressured to remain in them. Staff have agreed to monitor the situation closely and to share information with the safeguarding lead for further review.

Cadet disclosed that an older individual they met online has been asking for personal information and to meet in person. Safeguarding procedures should be followed without delay, with a clear record of actions and decisions taken.

Cadet has been observed winding up younger cadets and ignoring reminders about behaviour standards. The case will be escalated to the appropriate safeguarding officer and external agencies may need to be consulted.

Cadet disclosed that a Cadet Force Adult Volunteer (CFAV) has made them feel uncomfortable with comments and unnecessary physical contact during activities. Contact with external safeguarding agencies and, if necessary, emergency services should be considered without delay.

Cadet hinted that they are being pressured to go to addresses with people they do not know well and feel unable to say no. Contact with external safeguarding agencies and, if necessary, emergency services should be considered without delay. The cadet has attended most recent parades but staff have noticed changes in presentation and demeanour.

Cadet hinted that there are ongoing problems between carers at home and that they often stay out late to avoid being there. The safeguarding lead must be informed immediately and emergency procedures may need to be initiated.

Cadet stated that a CFAV has been sending them personal messages outside normal communication channels and has hinted at meeting one-to-one. The case will be escalated to the appropriate safeguarding officer and external agencies may need to be consulted. The information has been logged on the safeguarding system and will be reviewed at the next supervision meeting.

Cadet disclosed that relatives have spoken about a ‘special procedure’ to be carried out when they visit family abroad and appeared very anxious about this. Further conversation with the cadet and parents/carers is needed to understand the situation and decide on next steps.

Cadet disclosed that a Cadet Force Adult Volunteer (CFAV) has made them feel uncomfortable with comments and unnecessary physical contact during activities. The safeguarding lead must be informed immediately and emergency procedures may need to be initiated.

Staff were told that the cadet has been missing from home overnight on several occasions and returns with unexplained money or items. Staff have agreed to monitor the situation closely and to share information with the safeguarding lead for further review.

Parent conversation suggested that the family is planning a trip where cultural practices may include procedures that pose a risk of serious harm. Staff have agreed to monitor the situation closely and to share information with the safeguarding lead for further review.

Cadet reported being added to group chats where explicit or disturbing content is shared, and feels pressured to remain in them. The concern appears low-level at this stage; staff will continue to observe and provide a safe space for the cadet to talk.
